query: What are the major phases, decision points and associated fees, if any, in the NYISO generation interconnection cluster study process?
final_answer:
Below is the NYISO **generation interconnection Cluster Study Process** at a high level, based on NYISO’s Attachment HH tariff language and NYISO’s Interconnection User’s Guide. I’m treating this as the **standard interconnection process for generating facilities under the new cluster study rules**. Sources: NYISO OATT Attachment HH §40.2 / §40.5 and NYISO Transmission Expansion and Interconnection User’s Guide §9. (NYISO tariff PDFs: https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/44769082/OATT%2040.2.pdf/7f52dd6a-784b-4f77-4ffd-4c7d692a7892 and https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/44769082/OATT%2040.5.pdf/63cf5159-748d-b042-f9c8-4804a5b4c859; User’s Guide: https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/3625950/UG-21-TEI+Guide-v2-0-Final.pdf/2c727b38-9b4f-1d29-d967-1736d37aca28?t=1722447395298)

## 1) Major phases in the Cluster Study Process

### A. Optional pre-application stage
- Before the formal application window, an entity may review the **Heatmap** and request a **Pre-Application Report**. (NYISO OATT Attachment HH §40.2.2 / §40.4; source: https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/44769082/OATT%2040.2.pdf/7f52dd6a-784b-4f77-4ffd-4c7d692a7892)

### B. Application Window
- NYISO opens an **Application Window** for the cluster study cycle.
- For the standard Cluster Study Process, the Application Window is **45 calendar days**; for the Transition Cluster Study it was **75 calendar days**. (NYISO OATT §40.5.3; source: https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/44769082/OATT%2040.5.pdf/63cf5159-748d-b042-f9c8-4804a5b4c859)
- The applicant submits the Interconnection Request or CRIS-only Request, required technical data, site control, etc. (NYISO OATT §40.5.5; source: same URL as above)

### C. Application validation / deficiency cure
- NYISO reviews the submission and the customer must **cure deficiencies** identified by NYISO, the Connecting Transmission Owner, or an Affected Transmission Owner. (NYISO OATT §40.2.2 / §40.5.7; source: https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/44769082/OATT%2040.2.pdf/7f52dd6a-784b-4f77-4ffd-4c7d692a7892)
- If the request is valid, it becomes part of the relevant cluster. (NYISO OATT §40.2.2; same source)

### D. Customer Engagement Window
- After the application window, NYISO enters the **Customer Engagement Window**.
- During this phase NYISO publishes the cluster member list, the Connecting Transmission Owner performs a **Physical Infeasibility Screening**, and NYISO holds a **Scoping Meeting**. (NYISO OATT §40.2.2 / §40.7.1–§40.7.4; source: https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/44769082/OATT%2040.2.pdf/7f52dd6a-784b-4f77-4ffd-4c7d692a7892)
- The standard Customer Engagement Window is **70 calendar days**; the transition version was **90 calendar days**. (NYISO User’s Guide §9 and NYISO transition bulletin; source: https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/3625950/UG-21-TEI+Guide-v2-0-Final.pdf/2c727b38-9b4f-1d29-d967-1736d37aca28?t=1722447395298 and https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/45683353/11b_Draft_Tech%20Bulletin_Interconnection_Cluster%20Study%20Overview.pdf/ff9c2b58-904b-75aa-6da7-5220ff588b1b)

### E. Phase 1 Entry Decision Period
- At the end of Customer Engagement, the customer must decide whether to **proceed to Phase 1** and post **Readiness Deposit 1**, or **withdraw**. (NYISO OATT §40.7.5; source: https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/44769082/OATT%2040.2.pdf/7f52dd6a-784b-4f77-4ffd-4c7d692a7892)
- This is the first major decision point in the process. (same source)
- The standard decision period is **5 business days**. (NYISO User’s Guide §9 / NYISO transition materials; source: https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/3625950/UG-21-TEI+Guide-v2-0-Final.pdf/2c727b38-9b4f-1d29-d967-1736d37aca28?t=1722447395298)

### F. Phase 1 Study
- Phase 1 identifies the **Connecting Transmission Owner’s Attachment Facilities**, **Distribution Upgrades**, and **Local System Upgrade Facilities** required for reliable interconnection, and provides cost estimates and a preliminary construction schedule. (NYISO OATT §40.10.4; source: https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/44769082/OATT%2040.2.pdf/7f52dd6a-784b-4f77-4ffd-4c7d692a7892)
- The Phase 1 Study concludes when the Operating Committee approves the **Phase 1 Cost Estimates Summary Report**. (same source)
- The standard Phase 1 duration is **180 days**; the Transition Cluster Phase 1 duration was **190 days**. (NYISO User’s Guide §9 / NYISO transition bulletin; source: https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/3625950/UG-21-TEI+Guide-v2-0-Final.pdf/2c727b38-9b4f-1d29-d967-1736d37aca28?t=1722447395298 and https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/45683353/11b_Draft_Tech%20Bulletin_Interconnection_Cluster%20Study%20Overview.pdf/ff9c2b58-904b-75aa-6da7-5220ff588b1b)

### G. Phase 2 Entry Decision Period
- After Phase 1, the customer must decide whether to **proceed to Phase 2** and post **Readiness Deposit 2**, or **withdraw**. (NYISO OATT §40.10.8; source: https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/44769082/OATT%2040.2.pdf/7f52dd6a-784b-4f77-4ffd-4c7d692a7892)
- This is the second major decision point. (same source)
- The standard decision period is **10 business days**. (NYISO User’s Guide §9 / NYISO transition materials; source: https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/3625950/UG-21-TEI+Guide-v2-0-Final.pdf/2c727b38-9b4f-1d29-d967-1736d37aca28?t=1722447395298)

### H. Phase 2 Study
- Phase 2 identifies the **System Upgrade Facilities** and **Distribution Upgrades** needed under the NYISO Minimum Interconnection Standard. (NYISO OATT §40.11–§40.12; source: https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/44769082/OATT%2040.2.pdf/7f52dd6a-784b-4f77-4ffd-4c7d692a7892)
- If the project requests CRIS, NYISO also performs the **Cluster Study Deliverability Study** to identify any required **System Deliverability Upgrades (SDUs)**. (NYISO OATT §40.13; same source)
- Phase 2 concludes with Operating Committee approval of the **Cluster Study Report**. (NYISO OATT §40.11; same source)
- The standard Phase 2 duration is **270 days**. (NYISO User’s Guide §9 / NYISO transition materials; source: https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/3625950/UG-21-TEI+Guide-v2-0-Final.pdf/2c727b38-9b4f-1d29-d967-1736d37aca28?t=1722447395298 and https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/45683353/11b_Draft_Tech%20Bulletin_Interconnection_Cluster%20Study%20Overview.pdf/ff9c2b58-904b-75aa-6da7-5220ff588b1b)

### I. Final Decision Period
- After Phase 2, each customer enters an **iterative Final Decision Period** to accept its **Project Cost Allocation** and pay cash or post Security for the allocated facilities/upgrades. (NYISO OATT §40.15; source: https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/44769082/OATT%2040.2.pdf/7f52dd6a-784b-4f77-4ffd-4c7d692a7892)
- If the customer accepts and satisfies the payment/security requirement, it proceeds to negotiation of the **Standard Interconnection Agreement** and any construction agreements. (NYISO OATT §40.15 and §40.21; same source)
- If it does not accept or does not post the required amount, the project is withdrawn and may be subject to a withdrawal penalty. (same source)

## 2) Key decision points

1. **Apply or not apply during the Application Window**. If no valid request is submitted in the window, the project does not enter the cluster study. (NYISO OATT §40.5.4–§40.5.5; source: https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/44769082/OATT%2040.5.pdf/63cf5159-748d-b042-f9c8-4804a5b4c859)
2. **Cure deficiencies or fail validation**. An invalid/incomplete request must be cured timely or the project can be removed. (NYISO OATT §40.5.7; source: same URL)
3. **After Customer Engagement: proceed to Phase 1 or withdraw**. (NYISO OATT §40.7.5; source: https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/44769082/OATT%2040.2.pdf/7f52dd6a-784b-4f77-4ffd-4c7d692a7892)
4. **After Phase 1: proceed to Phase 2 or withdraw**. (NYISO OATT §40.10.8; same source)
5. **After Phase 2: accept cost allocation / post Security or withdraw**. (NYISO OATT §40.15; same source)
6. **If applicable, decide whether to pursue an Additional SDU Study** when deliverability analysis identifies new SDUs. (NYISO OATT §40.14 and §40.15; source: same URL)

## 3) Associated fees, deposits, and penalties

### Up-front application fee
- **$10,000 non-refundable Application Fee** for an Interconnection Request. (NYISO OATT §40.5.5.1.3; source: https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/44769082/OATT%2040.5.pdf/63cf5159-748d-b042-f9c8-4804a5b4c859)
- **$5,000** for a **CRIS-only** project. (same source)

### Study Deposit
- **$100,000** for a facility **< 80 MW**. (NYISO OATT §40.5.5.1.4; source: same URL)
- **$150,000** for a facility **≥ 80 MW and < 200 MW**. (same source)
- **$250,000** for a facility **≥ 200 MW**. (same source)
- **$50,000** for a **CRIS-only** request. (NYISO User’s Guide §6 and transition bulletin; source: https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/3625950/UG-21-TEI+Guide-v2-0-Final.pdf/2c727b38-9b4f-1d29-d967-1736d37aca28?t=1722447395298 and https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/1396587/Notice-of-the-Transition-Cluster-Study-Application-Window.pdf/ebbdb6b8-5cdd-f9fc-db90-adf2a107bb0d)

### Readiness Deposit 1
- **$4,000 per MW** to enter Phase 1. (NYISO transition materials and User’s Guide §9; source: https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/45683353/11b_Draft_Tech%20Bulletin_Interconnection_Cluster%20Study%20Overview.pdf/ff9c2b58-904b-75aa-6da7-5220ff588b1b and https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/3625950/UG-21-TEI+Guide-v2-0-Final.pdf/2c727b38-9b4f-1d29-d967-1736d37aca28?t=1722447395298)

### Readiness Deposit 2
- The amount is the **greater of**:
  - **Readiness Deposit 1**, and
  - **20% of the Phase 1 cost estimate** for Local SUF and CTOAF. (NYISO OATT §40.10.8 and NYISO User’s Guide §9; source: https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/44769082/OATT%2040.2.pdf/7f52dd6a-784b-4f77-4ffd-4c7d692a7892 and https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/3625950/UG-21-TEI+Guide-v2-0-Final.pdf/2c727b38-9b4f-1d29-d967-1736d37aca28?t=1722447395298)

### Withdrawal penalties
- **WP1:** If a project withdraws during the Customer Engagement Window or at the Phase 1 entry decision, it forfeits **25% of the Study Deposit**. (NYISO transition materials / tariff commentary; source: https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/45683353/11b_Draft_Tech%20Bulletin_Interconnection_Cluster%20Study%20Overview.pdf/ff9c2b58-904b-75aa-6da7-5220ff588b1b)
- **WP2:** If it withdraws during Phase 1 or at the end of Phase 1, it forfeits **50% of the Study Deposit + 10% of Readiness Deposit 1**. (same source)
- **WP3:** If it withdraws during Phase 2 or at the end of Phase 2, it forfeits **100% of the Study Deposit + 20% of Readiness Deposit 2**. (same source)
- NYISO also states there is **no withdrawal penalty** if withdrawal occurs during the Application Window, or within **5 business days** of the cluster member list being published, and no penalty for certain contingent projects or physical infeasibility cases. (NYISO tariff discussion materials; source: https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/43564982/Order%20No.%202023%

steps.content:
Below is the NYISO **generation interconnection Cluster Study Process** at a high level, based on NYISO’s Attachment HH tariff language and NYISO’s Interconnection User’s Guide. I’m treating this as the **standard interconnection process for generating facilities under the new cluster study rules**. Sources: NYISO OATT Attachment HH §40.2 / §40.5 and NYISO Transmission Expansion and Interconnection User’s Guide §9. (NYISO tariff PDFs: https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/44769082/OATT%2040.2.pdf/7f52dd6a-784b-4f77-4ffd-4c7d692a7892 and https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/44769082/OATT%2040.5.pdf/63cf5159-748d-b042-f9c8-4804a5b4c859; User’s Guide: https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/3625950/UG-21-TEI+Guide-v2-0-Final.pdf/2c727b38-9b4f-1d29-d967-1736d37aca28?t=1722447395298)

## 1) Major phases in the Cluster Study Process

### A. Optional pre-application stage
- Before the formal application window, an entity may review the **Heatmap** and request a **Pre-Application Report**. (NYISO OATT Attachment HH §40.2.2 / §40.4; source: https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/44769082/OATT%2040.2.pdf/7f52dd6a-784b-4f77-4ffd-4c7d692a7892)

### B. Application Window
- NYISO opens an **Application Window** for the cluster study cycle.
- For the standard Cluster Study Process, the Application Window is **45 calendar days**; for the Transition Cluster Study it was **75 calendar days**. (NYISO OATT §40.5.3; source: https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/44769082/OATT%2040.5.pdf/63cf5159-748d-b042-f9c8-4804a5b4c859)
- The applicant submits the Interconnection Request or CRIS-only Request, required technical data, site control, etc. (NYISO OATT §40.5.5; source: same URL as above)

### C. Application validation / deficiency cure
- NYISO reviews the submission and the customer must **cure deficiencies** identified by NYISO, the Connecting Transmission Owner, or an Affected Transmission Owner. (NYISO OATT §40.2.2 / §40.5.7; source: https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/44769082/OATT%2040.2.pdf/7f52dd6a-784b-4f77-4ffd-4c7d692a7892)
- If the request is valid, it becomes part of the relevant cluster. (NYISO OATT §40.2.2; same source)

### D. Customer Engagement Window
- After the application window, NYISO enters the **Customer Engagement Window**.
- During this phase NYISO publishes the cluster member list, the Connecting Transmission Owner performs a **Physical Infeasibility Screening**, and NYISO holds a **Scoping Meeting**. (NYISO OATT §40.2.2 / §40.7.1–§40.7.4; source: https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/44769082/OATT%2040.2.pdf/7f52dd6a-784b-4f77-4ffd-4c7d692a7892)
- The standard Customer Engagement Window is **70 calendar days**; the transition version was **90 calendar days**. (NYISO User’s Guide §9 and NYISO transition bulletin; source: https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/3625950/UG-21-TEI+Guide-v2-0-Final.pdf/2c727b38-9b4f-1d29-d967-1736d37aca28?t=1722447395298 and https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/45683353/11b_Draft_Tech%20Bulletin_Interconnection_Cluster%20Study%20Overview.pdf/ff9c2b58-904b-75aa-6da7-5220ff588b1b)

### E. Phase 1 Entry Decision Period
- At the end of Customer Engagement, the customer must decide whether to **proceed to Phase 1** and post **Readiness Deposit 1**, or **withdraw**. (NYISO OATT §40.7.5; source: https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/44769082/OATT%2040.2.pdf/7f52dd6a-784b-4f77-4ffd-4c7d692a7892)
- This is the first major decision point in the process. (same source)
- The standard decision period is **5 business days**. (NYISO User’s Guide §9 / NYISO transition materials; source: https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/3625950/UG-21-TEI+Guide-v2-0-Final.pdf/2c727b38-9b4f-1d29-d967-1736d37aca28?t=1722447395298)

### F. Phase 1 Study
- Phase 1 identifies the **Connecting Transmission Owner’s Attachment Facilities**, **Distribution Upgrades**, and **Local System Upgrade Facilities** required for reliable interconnection, and provides cost estimates and a preliminary construction schedule. (NYISO OATT §40.10.4; source: https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/44769082/OATT%2040.2.pdf/7f52dd6a-784b-4f77-4ffd-4c7d692a7892)
- The Phase 1 Study concludes when the Operating Committee approves the **Phase 1 Cost Estimates Summary Report**. (same source)
- The standard Phase 1 duration is **180 days**; the Transition Cluster Phase 1 duration was **190 days**. (NYISO User’s Guide §9 / NYISO transition bulletin; source: https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/3625950/UG-21-TEI+Guide-v2-0-Final.pdf/2c727b38-9b4f-1d29-d967-1736d37aca28?t=1722447395298 and https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/45683353/11b_Draft_Tech%20Bulletin_Interconnection_Cluster%20Study%20Overview.pdf/ff9c2b58-904b-75aa-6da7-5220ff588b1b)

### G. Phase 2 Entry Decision Period
- After Phase 1, the customer must decide whether to **proceed to Phase 2** and post **Readiness Deposit 2**, or **withdraw**. (NYISO OATT §40.10.8; source: https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/44769082/OATT%2040.2.pdf/7f52dd6a-784b-4f77-4ffd-4c7d692a7892)
- This is the second major decision point. (same source)
- The standard decision period is **10 business days**. (NYISO User’s Guide §9 / NYISO transition materials; source: https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/3625950/UG-21-TEI+Guide-v2-0-Final.pdf/2c727b38-9b4f-1d29-d967-1736d37aca28?t=1722447395298)

### H. Phase 2 Study
- Phase 2 identifies the **System Upgrade Facilities** and **Distribution Upgrades** needed under the NYISO Minimum Interconnection Standard. (NYISO OATT §40.11–§40.12; source: https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/44769082/OATT%2040.2.pdf/7f52dd6a-784b-4f77-4ffd-4c7d692a7892)
- If the project requests CRIS, NYISO also performs the **Cluster Study Deliverability Study** to identify any required **System Deliverability Upgrades (SDUs)**. (NYISO OATT §40.13; same source)
- Phase 2 concludes with Operating Committee approval of the **Cluster Study Report**. (NYISO OATT §40.11; same source)
- The standard Phase 2 duration is **270 days**. (NYISO User’s Guide §9 / NYISO transition materials; source: https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/3625950/UG-21-TEI+Guide-v2-0-Final.pdf/2c727b38-9b4f-1d29-d967-1736d37aca28?t=1722447395298 and https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/45683353/11b_Draft_Tech%20Bulletin_Interconnection_Cluster%20Study%20Overview.pdf/ff9c2b58-904b-75aa-6da7-5220ff588b1b)

### I. Final Decision Period
- After Phase 2, each customer enters an **iterative Final Decision Period** to accept its **Project Cost Allocation** and pay cash or post Security for the allocated facilities/upgrades. (NYISO OATT §40.15; source: https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/44769082/OATT%2040.2.pdf/7f52dd6a-784b-4f77-4ffd-4c7d692a7892)
- If the customer accepts and satisfies the payment/security requirement, it proceeds to negotiation of the **Standard Interconnection Agreement** and any construction agreements. (NYISO OATT §40.15 and §40.21; same source)
- If it does not accept or does not post the required amount, the project is withdrawn and may be subject to a withdrawal penalty. (same source)

## 2) Key decision points

1. **Apply or not apply during the Application Window**. If no valid request is submitted in the window, the project does not enter the cluster study. (NYISO OATT §40.5.4–§40.5.5; source: https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/44769082/OATT%2040.5.pdf/63cf5159-748d-b042-f9c8-4804a5b4c859)
2. **Cure deficiencies or fail validation**. An invalid/incomplete request must be cured timely or the project can be removed. (NYISO OATT §40.5.7; source: same URL)
3. **After Customer Engagement: proceed to Phase 1 or withdraw**. (NYISO OATT §40.7.5; source: https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/44769082/OATT%2040.2.pdf/7f52dd6a-784b-4f77-4ffd-4c7d692a7892)
4. **After Phase 1: proceed to Phase 2 or withdraw**. (NYISO OATT §40.10.8; same source)
5. **After Phase 2: accept cost allocation / post Security or withdraw**. (NYISO OATT §40.15; same source)
6. **If applicable, decide whether to pursue an Additional SDU Study** when deliverability analysis identifies new SDUs. (NYISO OATT §40.14 and §40.15; source: same URL)

## 3) Associated fees, deposits, and penalties

### Up-front application fee
- **$10,000 non-refundable Application Fee** for an Interconnection Request. (NYISO OATT §40.5.5.1.3; source: https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/44769082/OATT%2040.5.pdf/63cf5159-748d-b042-f9c8-4804a5b4c859)
- **$5,000** for a **CRIS-only** project. (same source)

### Study Deposit
- **$100,000** for a facility **< 80 MW**. (NYISO OATT §40.5.5.1.4; source: same URL)
- **$150,000** for a facility **≥ 80 MW and < 200 MW**. (same source)
- **$250,000** for a facility **≥ 200 MW**. (same source)
- **$50,000** for a **CRIS-only** request. (NYISO User’s Guide §6 and transition bulletin; source: https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/3625950/UG-21-TEI+Guide-v2-0-Final.pdf/2c727b38-9b4f-1d29-d967-1736d37aca28?t=1722447395298 and https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/1396587/Notice-of-the-Transition-Cluster-Study-Application-Window.pdf/ebbdb6b8-5cdd-f9fc-db90-adf2a107bb0d)

### Readiness Deposit 1
- **$4,000 per MW** to enter Phase 1. (NYISO transition materials and User’s Guide §9; source: https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/45683353/11b_Draft_Tech%20Bulletin_Interconnection_Cluster%20Study%20Overview.pdf/ff9c2b58-904b-75aa-6da7-5220ff588b1b and https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/3625950/UG-21-TEI+Guide-v2-0-Final.pdf/2c727b38-9b4f-1d29-d967-1736d37aca28?t=1722447395298)

### Readiness Deposit 2
- The amount is the **greater of**:
  - **Readiness Deposit 1**, and
  - **20% of the Phase 1 cost estimate** for Local SUF and CTOAF. (NYISO OATT §40.10.8 and NYISO User’s Guide §9; source: https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/44769082/OATT%2040.2.pdf/7f52dd6a-784b-4f77-4ffd-4c7d692a7892 and https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/3625950/UG-21-TEI+Guide-v2-0-Final.pdf/2c727b38-9b4f-1d29-d967-1736d37aca28?t=1722447395298)

### Withdrawal penalties
- **WP1:** If a project withdraws during the Customer Engagement Window or at the Phase 1 entry decision, it forfeits **25% of the Study Deposit**. (NYISO transition materials / tariff commentary; source: https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/45683353/11b_Draft_Tech%20Bulletin_Interconnection_Cluster%20Study%20Overview.pdf/ff9c2b58-904b-75aa-6da7-5220ff588b1b)
- **WP2:** If it withdraws during Phase 1 or at the end of Phase 1, it forfeits **50% of the Study Deposit + 10% of Readiness Deposit 1**. (same source)
- **WP3:** If it withdraws during Phase 2 or at the end of Phase 2, it forfeits **100% of the Study Deposit + 20% of Readiness Deposit 2**. (same source)
- NYISO also states there is **no withdrawal penalty** if withdrawal occurs during the Application Window, or within **5 business days** of the cluster member list being published, and no penalty for certain contingent projects or physical infeasibility cases. (NYISO tariff discussion materials; source: https://www.nyiso.com/documents/20142/43564982/Order%20No.%202023%
